channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Waratahs lose to Blues after strong first-half showing
item.description: The Waratahs suffered their 12th consecutive defeat to the Blues, despite dominating much of the first half. Leading 17-8 at halftime, the NSW side had several chances to extend their lead but repeate...

item.title: Akhilesh Yadav criticises BJP after cow vigilante's death in Mathura, raises law and order concern
item.description: Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav criticised the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) over the death of a self-styled cow vigilante known as Farsa Baba in Mathura, Uttar Pradesh. Allegedly, the ...

item.title: Muslims worldwide celebrate Eidul Fitr 1447 AH with prayers and festivities
item.description: Muslims across various countries welcomed Eidul Fitr 1447 AH on March 21, 2026, marking the conclusion of the holy month of Ramazan. This important Islamic festival is observed with communal prayers, ...

item.title: Rhoda Roberts, influential Indigenous cultural leader, passes away at 66
item.description: Rhoda Roberts, a pioneering figure in Indigenous culture, media, and the arts, has passed away at the age of 66 after a battle with cancer. The Bundjalung woman is credited with coining the term 'Welc...

item.title: Syd Hartha Returns to Rappler Live Jam with New Single 'Ako Naman Muna'
item.description: Acclaimed singer-songwriter syd hartha is set to return to Rappler Live Jam on Saturday, March 21, 2026, to showcase her new single, 'ako naman muna.' The folk-pop artist, known for her honest and vul...

item.title: Cyberattack Disables Car Breathalyzers, Leaving Drivers Stranded
item.description: A cyberattack on Intoxalock, a company providing breathalyzers for vehicles, has left many drivers stranded, unable to start their cars. The devices, which are required for some individuals due to cou...

item.title: Broos' Bafana squad selection raises debate over key omissions
item.description: As South Africa prepares for two crucial warm-up matches ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, the exclusion of Orlando Pirates goalkeeper Sipho Chaine has caused a stir among fans and analysts. Despite a...

item.title: Doja Cat Delivers Stunning Performance at Global Citizen’s Move Afrika 2026 in Pretoria
item.description: Doja Cat performed in Pretoria for the first time on March 20, 2026, as part of the Global Citizen Move Afrika 2026 tour, electrifying the crowd at the SunBet Arena. The event not only celebrated her ...

item.title: Nairobi Businesswoman Supports Kayole Girl Caring for Siblings and Confronts Harassment
item.description: Elizabeth Nzisa, a young girl from Kayole, became the sole provider for her three siblings after losing her mother and being abandoned by her father. Her story went viral after an emotional interview ...

item.title: Two die in Lekki–Epe road accident involving gas tanker and tipper truck
item.description: In the early morning of Saturday, two men sadly lose their lives for a serious road accident wey happen for Lekki–Epe Expressway, Lagos State. The tori talk say the two victims, wey be the driver of o...

item.title: BJP praises Shashi Tharoor's balanced stance on Iran war, takes a dig at Rahul Gandhi
item.description: Following Congress MP Shashi Tharoor’s endorsement of India's measured approach to the Iran crisis, the BJP seized the opportunity to praise Tharoor while criticizing opposition leader Rahul Gandhi’s ...

item.title: Manchester City and Arsenal set for Carabao Cup Final showdown at Wembley
item.description: This Sunday, Manchester City go face Arsenal for the Carabao Cup final wey go hold for Wembley Stadium. City never too dey perform this season, as Arsenal don dey shine with one of their best campaign...

item.title: Western Media Bias and the Human Cost of the Iran Strikes
item.description: Muna Khan’s article in Dawn, titled 'Whose Fear Counts?', offers a moving reflection on how Western media reports wars differently depending on whose suffering is being shown. She shares the stories o...

item.title: Mother and child die after electricity pole collapses in Malindi, investigation underway
item.description: In Malindi, a mother and her young child have died after a power pole collapsed on them, prompting an outcry from residents who are blaming Kenya Power for poor maintenance of the electricity infrastr...

item.title: ABS-CBN releases poster for upcoming action-drama series 'Blood vs Duty'
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios has officially revealed the poster for its latest television offering, the action-drama series “Blood vs Duty.” Starring some of the country’s top talents—Richard Gutierrez, Bela Padil...

item.title: Letter from Dar es Salaam: Finding kindness amid the floods
item.description: In this heartfelt letter written by Anthony Tambwe from Dar es Salaam to his nephew Milambo in Ukumbisiganga, the author reflects deeply on the contrast between city life and the serenity of the villa...

item.title: Makhadzi and Upcoming Artist Blissbouy Owfive Spark Dating Rumours After Shared Video
item.description: A short video clip showing South African musician Makhadzi sharing a kiss with up-and-coming artist Blissbouy Owfive has stirred up excitement and speculation among fans. The video, originally posted ...

item.title: New evidence suggests King Harold sailed rather than marched to the Battle of Hastings
item.description: New research challenges one of the most enduring stories in English history: the notion that King Harold Godwinson undertook an almost miraculous forced march from York to Hastings in 1066. Traditiona...

item.title: BTS Song 'Body to Body' Features 120 BPM Tempo Ideal for Hands-Only CPR, Says American Heart Association
item.description: BTS recently released their new track 'Body to Body' as the opening pop-rap song in their fifth studio album titled 'ARIRANG,' marking a significant comeback for the group. The American Heart Associat...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Enters Third Week with Escalation, Limited Allied Support and Rising Economic Impact
item.description: The US-Iran war, which began on February 28 under President Donald Trump, has now entered its third week and appears to be spiralling out of his full control. Despite claims of military success, inclu...
